# RustyNES v2.0.2 — "Harbor"

**The second release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train — and Harbor's headline accuracy release.** The two new upstream AccuracyCoin PPU tests that v2.0.1 documented as honest gaps — **"ALE + Read"** and **"Hybrid Addresses"** — are now **solved flag-on**: a whole-dot port of TriCNES's octal-latch multiplexed-bus model (**ADR 0030**), behind the default-off `mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid` feature flag, reaches **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)**.

Per the *Feature-Flag Additive Change* + "bake, then promote" guardrails, **v2.0.2 ships the model default-off.** The shipped default build is **byte-identical to v2.0.1** and holds its honest **139/141 (98.58%)**; the experimental flag-on model is verified at **141/141**. Promotion to default (shipped 141/141) is the deliberate **v2.0.3** step. No store submission yet (that is v2.1.0); Android continues as GitHub-sideload.

---

## The octal-latch multiplexed-bus PPU model (ADR 0030, commit `27c103c`)

To save pins, the real 2C02 PPU multiplexes its low eight VRAM **address** pins with the eight VRAM **data** pins (PA0-7 = AD7-0). Every VRAM access therefore takes two PPU cycles, and an external **octal latch** (74LS373-class) captures A7-A0 on the ALE-high cycle and supplies them back on the ALE-low cycle. When the address halves desync, the PPU reads from a **hybrid address** it never coherently output — the mechanism the two new tests exercise.

Behind the pre-existing default-off `mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid` flag (`rustynes-ppu` → forwarded through `rustynes-core` and `rustynes-test-harness`), the reworked model adds an 8-bit `octal_latch`, a 14-bit `address_bus`, and a `copy_v` / `pattern_latch_stale` transient pair (all `#[cfg]`-gated). Every background fetch resolves its effective VRAM address through `octal_effective` — the TriCNES `FetchPPU` splice `(address_bus & 0x3F00) | octal_latch` — which is **transparent** (returns the intended address, reloads the latch) except on the two modeled corruption events:

- **"ALE + Read"** (`$0491`): a mid-render `$2007` (PPUDATA) read whose 3-cycle state-machine countdown lands during rendering freezes `octal_latch` on the read's DATA byte; the next pattern fetch reads `{PAR high 6}:{stale $FF}` = `$0FFF`, lighting eight visible pixels over a transparent tile. Verified per-dot: `$2007`R @ scanline 3 dot 223 → latch = `$FF` @ dot 228 → pattern read `$0FFF` @ dot 230.
- **"Hybrid Addresses"** (`$0492`): a mid-fetch `$2006` second write during rendering sets `copy_v` and captures the stale octal-latch low byte; the next nametable fetch splices `{new v high $2F00}:{stale $19}` = `$2F19`, lighting one pixel a sprite-zero hit detects. Verified: `$2006`W2 @ scanline 4 dot 182 → hybrid NT read `$2F19` @ dot 186.

A12 / MMC3 notification stays on the **intended** (un-spliced) fetch address at every call site, so fetch-address / A12 IRQ timing is **unchanged**. No snapshot-format bump is needed — the latch fields self-heal on the next in-blanking fetch ALE and the `copy_v` / `pattern_latch_stale` one-shots are consumed within a few dots — so `PPU_SNAPSHOT_VERSION` stays at 4.

## Oracle-first discipline: the oracle was wrong (ADR 0030 resolution)

The campaign (`docs/audit/v2.0.2-octal-latch-campaign-2026-07-08.md`) corrected two premises v2.0.1 carried into ADR 0030:

1. **Mesen2 does NOT pass these two tests.** The per-cycle bus cross-diff proved the vendored Mesen2 build reads `0x0A` (corruption not reproduced) on both — so ADR 0030's prior "Option 2 = proven-correct Mesen2 recipe" premise was false. The correct definitive oracle is **TriCNES** (`ref-proj/TriCNES/Emulator.cs`, MIT, commit `9199870` — the **AccuracyCoin author's own emulator**), which models the multiplexed AD/A bus + octal latch at transistor level and does drive `$2F19` / `$0FFF`.
2. **A whole-dot port suffices.** The full 2-cycle-ALE fetch refactor (v2.0.1's Option 1) was **not** required; porting TriCNES's octal latch at RustyNES's whole-dot cadence, behind the existing flag, reaches 141/141 flag-on.

## Verification — the honesty bar

- **Flag-OFF (shipped default):** byte-identical to v2.0.1 — AccuracyCoin **139/141 (98.58%)**, nestest 0-diff, mmc3 + `ppu_sprites` all pass. The two "ALE + Read" / "Hybrid Addresses" tests remain the honest measured gaps in the shipped build.
- **Flag-ON (`mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid`, experimental):** AccuracyCoin **141/141 (100.00%)**, framebuffer 100%, **no regression** — nestest 0-diff, mmc3 (A12 clocking + IRQ) all pass, `ppu_sprites` 19/19, `mmc1_a12` pass.

## Refine-then-promote — the v2.0.3 plan (maintainer decision)

The model ships **flag-off** in v2.0.2 for two reasons: the 60-ROM commercial byte-identity oracle (ADR 0030's mandated promote-safety gate) is maintainer-run and could not be exercised at implementation time (no local dumps), and the Hybrid path carries a `+1 coarse-X` latch-capture approximation (exact for the tested alignment). **v2.0.3** reworks that path to a **first-principles latch-carry model** (the `octal_latch` naturally holding the stale low byte across dots, per TriCNES's ALE-driven latch), then — gated on the 60-ROM commercial oracle flag-on + broader `$2007`/`$2006`-during-render title validation + a CI job asserting flag-on 141/141 — **promotes the flag to default** (shipped AccuracyCoin 141/141), weighing the ADR 0028 save-state / byte-identity implication at that point.

**This release does not claim the shipped build is 141/141, and does not claim the flag has been promoted.**

## Dependencies

No dependency changes this release (the v2.0.1 sweep — uniffi 0.32 / mlua 0.12 / wgpu-naga 29.0.4 / cc 1.2.66 — carries forward; wgpu/naga 30 remains deferred on the egui 0.35 pin).

---

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.3** — the octal-latch Hybrid first-principles rework + the 60-ROM oracle gate → **promote `mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid` to default** (shipped 141/141).
- **v2.0.4** — Android feature-parity finalization + foss/play on-device verification + monetization wiring.
- **v2.0.5–v2.0.8** — iOS finalization on the v2.0.0 core.
- **v2.0.9** — joint readiness verification for both apps.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch**.
